name: Scorer Validation Relevance review for Ssr Assessment Stakes Boundaries bundle
summary: Accepted. The claim is relevant to scorer validation because it defines when synthetic responses can be used around validation, calibration, augmentation, and stress testing, while explicitly saying that lower-stakes formative examples and contextual guidance are not final scorer-validation evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: Watts et al. concerns formative chatbot prompt and feedback testing rather than automated scorer validation.
findings.why_it_matters: This source should not be used to support claims about scorer deployment readiness.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the Watts support limited to formative prototype testing, as currently written.
